rag_rules: Insert generated AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when referring to component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Ensure that the inserted AGREE annex blocks are placed in the correct scope for the intended purpose
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions and guarantees in implementation scopes when referring to component ports
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
